title: Output messages display rawInput
body:
<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

`init`, `checkpint/restore` and `cleanup` command now display
output messages which is rawInput instead of a container ID.

Example:
```
$ podman init <container name>
<container name>

$ podman init <short container ID>
<short container ID>
```

Signed-off-by: Toshiki Sonoda <sonoda.toshiki@fujitsu.com>

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Now output messages display rawInput instead of a container ID.
```

comments.body: I don't know if we can do this by default, it will break Docker compatibility (and this feels like one of the places where it's very likely that scripts - including our own test suite - are parsing the output of the `podman create` and `podman run -d` command literally, and we'd definitely break them).

> I don't know if we can do this by default, it will break Docker compatibility (and this feels like one of the places where it's very likely that scripts - including our own test suite - are parsing the output of the `podman create` and `podman run -d` command literally, and we'd definitely break them).

I understand. I'll try not to modify `create` and `run`.
For other commands about `rm`, `stop` and so on... , I would like to modify the output message that the container name is displayed by `--all` or `--latest`. What do you think?

Example:
```
$ podman rm -a
ctr_name1
ctr_name2
ctr_name3
```
Can you break this out into two PRs one for the podman rm --filter and one for the CID Files.
As you can tell by the number of changes you're making to the test suite, this is an ultra-breaking change. Even just changing `rm` and `stop` would cause chaos in the field.

Certainly. This change has too much impact...
I will modify output message to show `rawInput` for the moment.
Currently, `start/stop`, `pause/unpause` and `kill` are already displayed `rawInput` (Also, `rm` and `restart` will be reflected by #15084  and #14999 ).
Therefore, I'll try to modify the other commands (`cleanup`, `checkpoint/restore`, `init`).
What is the docker behaviour? We should not make breaking changes for no reasons.

> What is the docker behaviour? We should not make breaking changes for no reasons.

I verified the command `run`, `restart`, `start`, `stop`, `pause`, `unpause` outputs `rawInput`, at least.

<details>
<summary>docker command by name</summary>

```
# docker run -d --name test nginx
f4b27a7a3bf84b1803eb00e70738dbc12c3a8ae5a41ce452f4aafcaabba6224e
# docker restart test
test
# docker stop test
test
# docker start test
test
# docker pause test
test
# docker unpause test
test
# docker rm -f test
test
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>docker command by short ID</summary>

```
# docker run -d nginx
1960031807c74adfc82b91dc4adf03c9d657ebad5e171f9a119c599ee73575a0
# docker restart 19600
19600
# docker stop 19600
19600
# docker start 19600
19600
# docker pause 19600
19600
# docker unpause 19600
19600
# docker rm -f 19600
19600
```
</details>


`docker checkpoint` outputs `checkpoint name`.
However, `podman container checkpoint` does not have the concept of `checkpoint name`.


<details>
<summary>docker checkpoint by name</summary>

```
# docker run -d --name test nginx
62009aad0bdc976c7b7d64e1136deca849803ea4ee96bd107d1cb1a25ea8832d
# docker checkpoint create test cp
cp

# docker stop test
test
# docker ps -a
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE     COMMAND                  CREATED          STATUS                        PORTS     NAMES
62009aad0bdc   nginx     "/docker-entrypoint.…"   42 seconds ago   Exited (137) 32 seconds ago             test
# docker start --checkpoint cp test
<nothing outputs, is this a bug?>

# docker ps
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE     COMMAND                  CREATED          STATUS         PORTS     NAMES
62009aad0bdc   nginx     "/docker-entrypoint.…"   59 seconds ago   Up 3 seconds   80/tcp    test
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>docker checkpoint by short ID</summary>

```
# docker run -d nginx
d83bd9117e1cce39ef48a1107c8b9d676c25ab6a21b90cce3a4482755eb9975a
# docker checkpoint create d83b cp2
cp2

# docker stop d83b
d83b
# docker ps -a
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE     COMMAND                  CREATED          STATUS                        PORTS     NAMES
d83bd9117e1c   nginx     "/docker-entrypoint.…"   46 seconds ago   Exited (137) 17 seconds ago             sweet_cannon
# docker start --checkpoint cp2 d83b
<nothing outputs, is this a bug?>

# docker ps
CONTAINER ID   IMAGE     COMMAND                  CREATED              STATUS         PORTS     NAMES
d83bd9117e1c   nginx     "/docker-entrypoint.…"   About a minute ago   Up 4 seconds   80/tcp    sweet_cannon
```
</details>

To my understanding, Docker has no equivalent commands for `podman container cleanup` and `podman init`.
